hero.title: Sustainable Development through Local Value Creation in Africa
hero.subtitle: We promote climate-resilient livelihoods, food security and nature conservation through integrated, community-led projects

about.title: About Mimea Impact
about.intro: Mimea Impact is a Swiss non-profit association founded in 2026, dedicated to sustainable development in East Africa. We believe in an integrated approach that combines community development, food security, nature conservation, and climate resilience.
about.introText: Our work is guided by rigorous scientific principles, strong local ownership, and complete transparency. We collaborate closely with local communities, academic institutions, and partner organizations to create sustainable and measurable impact.
about.missionText: Our mission is to support local entrepreneurship and livelihoods through activities related to moringa and other local plant crops. We combine these economic opportunities with essential environmental objectives: biodiversity conservation and reducing conflicts between communities and wild animals.
about.boardIntro: Our board brings diverse expertise in governance, law, and international project management.
about.teamIntro: Our field team combines academic expertise with deep local knowledge for effective and culturally adapted implementation.

teamMember1.name: Fabrice Volken, MSc ETH
teamMember1.bio: Fabrice holds a Master's degree in Applied Natural Sciences from ETH Zurich. As a passionate social entrepreneur with over 15 years of experience, he has developed unique expertise in integrated rural development. He coordinates all Mimea Impact projects in Switzerland and Africa, and maintains strategic relationships with our local and international partners.

teamMember2.name: Esther Mrabu
teamMember2.bio: Esther brings over 8 years of experience in community mobilization and agricultural project coordination in Kilifi County. A native of the region, she has a deep understanding of local dynamics, languages, and traditions. Her extensive network and ability to build trust with communities are essential to the success of our interventions.

about.legalText: Association under Art. 60 ff. Swiss Civil Code | Headquarters: Adliswil, Zurich | Non-profit recognized

lumoDetail.title: Lumo+ | Conservation Tourism & Community Development
lumoDetail.challengeTitle: The Challenge: Transforming Conflicts into Opportunities
lumoDetail.challengePara1: In Kenya, communities living near conservation areas bear the high costs of coexistence with wildlife: crop destruction, threats to livestock, risks to human safety. Yet they rarely benefit from the revenue generated by conservation tourism.
lumoDetail.challengePara2: This economic injustice creates growing tensions between conservation and community development. Without viable economic alternatives, communities lose their motivation to protect wildlife, threatening Kenya's unique ecosystems.
lumoDetail.challengeBoxText: This project demonstrates that well-designed conservation tourism can create sustainable jobs, generate significant local revenue, and transform the perception of wildlife from "costly threat" to "valuable economic asset".
lumoDetail.approachTitle: Our Approach: A Sustainable Business Model
lumoDetail.approachSubtitle: Unlike traditional projects depending on continuous grants, Lumo+ establishes a self-sustaining business model based on three complementary pillars.
lumoDetail.approachHighlight: Each pillar generates revenue from year one and creates sustainable local jobs
lumoDetail.pillar1Desc: Professional safari operations led by an experienced and award-winning ranger. Generates immediate revenue and creates 15-20 skilled jobs (guides, drivers, reception staff).
lumoDetail.pillar1Benefits: ✓ Profitable from year 1 • ✓ Immediate direct jobs
lumoDetail.pillar2Desc: Low environmental impact accommodation infrastructure: renewable energy, rainwater harvesting, zero plastic. Significantly increases revenue capacity.
lumoDetail.pillar2Benefits: ✓ High environmental standards • ✓ Attracts premium tourism
lumoDetail.pillar3Desc: Agricultural diversification (drought-resistant sunflower), community bakery and beekeeping. Creates 30-40 additional jobs and improves local food security.
lumoDetail.pillar3Benefits: ✓ Stable agricultural income • ✓ Enhanced food security
lumoDetail.impactIntro: Lumo+ aims for complete economic transformation of the community conservancy in 4 years, with concrete and measurable results at each stage.
lumoDetail.impactJobsDetail: 15-20 in safaris, 10-15 at the lodge, 25-30 in agriculture
lumoDetail.impactRevenueDetail: From CHF 20'000 to CHF 180'000 annually for the community
lumoDetail.impact1Text: The project becomes financially independent after 4 years, eliminating dependence on donations
lumoDetail.impact2Text: Communities perceive wildlife as an economic asset, reducing conflicts
lumoDetail.impact3Text: Training in tourism management, professional guiding and agricultural entrepreneurship
lumoDetail.impact4Text: Creates a blueprint for other community conservancies in Kenya
lumoDetail.ctaText: Your support enables the creation of sustainable local jobs and promotes harmonious coexistence between communities and wildlife.

moringaDetail.title: Moringa Whole Value Chain | Climate, Nutrition, Water & Education
moringaDetail.challengePara1: In Ganze Sub-County, rural communities face an interconnected triple threat: climate change with prolonged droughts, chronic malnutrition particularly affecting children, and limited access to clean drinking water causing waterborne diseases and school absenteeism.
moringaDetail.challengePara2: Traditional agricultural methods are no longer viable in the face of climate change. Families lack affordable nutritious alternatives. Children walk kilometers to access often contaminated water, compromising their health and education.
moringaDetail.challengeBoxTitle: Why an integrated approach is essential:
moringaDetail.challengeBoxText: Moringa offers a unique systemic solution: drought-resistant, super-nutritious and a natural water purifier. Your support transforms this local plant into a pillar of resilience for 2'500 households and 12 schools.
moringaDetail.approachTitle: Our Approach: A Complete Value Chain
moringaDetail.approachSubtitle: This project establishes the region's first integrated Moringa value chain, linking agricultural production, nutrition, clean drinking water and science education. Each component reinforces the others for maximum systemic impact.
moringaDetail.approachHighlight: Scientifically founded through our partnerships with KEFRI, Pwani University and the World Bank
moringaDetail.componentA: Climate-resilient agriculture (Moringa Dual Cropping + Carbon Credits)
moringaDetail.componentADesc: Drought-resistant dual cropping with carbon credit potential. 2'500 households adopt climate-adapted agricultural practices.
moringaDetail.componentBDesc: Biodigester transforms waste into organic fertilizer, improving soil health and reducing agricultural costs.
moringaDetail.componentCDesc: Nutrition programs integrating Moringa into local diets, particularly to combat child malnutrition.
moringaDetail.componentDDesc: 10 water access points with natural purification using Moringa seeds, eliminating 90-99% of pathogenic bacteria.
moringaDetail.componentEDesc: Laboratory for quality control and practical farmer training, ensuring standards and knowledge transfer.
moringaDetail.componentFDesc: Educational program in 12 schools: school gardens, nutrition, water purification. Training the next generation.
moringaDetail.impactIntro: This project aims for systemic transformation simultaneously affecting climate resilience, nutritional health and access to clean drinking water. Each component generates measurable and sustainable benefits.
moringaDetail.impactSchoolsDetail: School gardens, nutrition and water purification
moringaDetail.impact1Text: Drought-resistant Moringa crop + double annual harvest. Strengthened food security in the face of climate change.
moringaDetail.impact2Text: Reduction in child malnutrition through access to an affordable local superfood (7× more vitamin C than an orange).
moringaDetail.impact3Text: 10 access points with natural purification eliminating 90-99% of bacteria. Reduced waterborne diseases and school absenteeism.
moringaDetail.impact4Text: Carbon credit potential via CO2 sequestration, creating an additional sustainable income source for farmers.
moringaDetail.impact5Text: Partnerships with KEFRI, Pwani University and World Bank-funded project ensure quality and sustainability.
moringaDetail.impact6Text: 12 schools with practical programs train the next generation in sustainable agricultural practices and nutrition.
moringaDetail.partner1: Kilifi Moringa Estate (World Bank Strategic Partner, DEG-financed)
moringaDetail.ctaText: Your contribution helps build climate resilience, improve nutrition and ensure access to clean drinking water for 2'500 households.
